label: 40728 Transfer of firearms, ammunition, and parts

children.text: all firearms on loan to affiliated clubs and State associations;
all firearms in the possession of the Civilian Marksmanship Support Detachment; and
all M–1 Garand and caliber .22 rimfire rifles stored at Defense Distribution Depot, Anniston, Anniston, Alabama.
to issue or loan firearms or ammunition under ; orsection 40731 of this title
to sell firearms or ammunition under .section 40732 of this title

children.heading: .—Vesting of Title in Transferred Items
children.label: c .—Vesting of Title in Transferred Items

children.text: on the issuance of the item to an eligible recipient under ; orsection 40731 of this title
immediately before the corporation delivers the item to a purchaser in accordance with a contract for sale of the item that is authorized under .section 40732 of this title

children.heading: .—Storage of Firearms
children.label: d .—Storage of Firearms
children.text: Firearms stored at Defense Distribution Depot, Anniston, Anniston, Alabama, before , and used for the Civilian Marksmanship Program (as that program existed under before ), shall remain at that facility or another storage facility designated by the Secretary, without cost to the corporation, until the firearms are issued, loaned, or sold by the corporation, or otherwise transferred to the corporation.February 10, 1996February 10, 1996section 4308(e) of title 10

children.heading: .—Discretionary Transfer of Parts
children.label: e .—Discretionary Transfer of Parts
children.text: The Secretary may transfer from the inventory of the Department of the Army to the corporation any part from a rifle designated to be demilitarized.

children.heading: M–1 .—Limitation on Demilitarization ofRifles
children.label: f M–1 .—Limitation on Demilitarization ofRifles
children.text: After , the Secretary may not demilitarize an M–1 Garand rifle in the inventory of the Army unless the Defense Logistics Agency decides the rifle is unserviceable.February 10, 1996

children.heading: .—Cost of Transfers
children.label: g .—Cost of Transfers
children.text: A transfer of firearms, ammunition, or parts to the corporation under this section shall be made without cost to the corporation, except that the corporation shall assume the cost of preparation and transportation of firearms and ammunition transferred under this section.

Subject to paragraph (2), the Secretary may transfer to the corporation, in accordance with the procedure prescribed in this subchapter, surplus caliber .45 M1911/M1911A1 pistols and spare parts and related accessories for those pistols that, on the date of the enactment of this subsection, are under the control of the Secretary and are surplus to the requirements of the Department of the Army, and such material as may be recovered by the Secretary pursuant to . The Secretary shall determine a reasonable schedule for the transfer of such surplus pistols.section 40728A(a) of this title

The Secretary may not transfer more than 10,000 surplus caliber .45 M1911/M1911A1 pistols to the corporation during any year and may only transfer such pistols as long as pistols described in paragraph (1) remain available for transfer.

children.label: A
children.text: on the date of the enactment of this paragraph are under the control of the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: are surplus to the requirements of the Department of the Army at the time of the submission of the report required in subsection (k).

The Secretary may not transfer pursuant to paragraph (3) any shotgun that is a modular ancillary addition to a service rifle, or meets the definition of a “short-barreled shotgun” as that term is defined in , United States Code.section 921(a)(6) of title 18

Notwithstanding subsections (a) and (b), the Secretary of the Navy may transfer to the corporation, in accordance with the procedures prescribed in this subchapter, M–1 Garand and caliber .22 rimfire rifles held within the inventories of the United States Navy and the United States Marine Corps and stored at Defense Distribution Depot, Anniston, Alabama, or Naval Surface Warfare Center, Crane, Indiana, as of the date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2018.

children.label: A
children.text: shall be used as awards for competitors in marksmanship competitions held by the United States Marine Corps or the United States Navy and may not be resold; and

children.label: B
children.text: shall be rendered inoperable prior to award and transfer to marksmanship competitors.

The Secretary may not transfer pursuant to paragraph (3) any shotgun that is a modular ancillary addition to a service rifle or meets the definition of a ‘short-barreled shotgun’ as that term is defined in , United States Code.section 921(a)(6) of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: on the date of the enactment of this paragraph are under the control of the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: are surplus to the requirements of the Department of the Air Force at the time of the submission of the report required in subsection (k).

The Secretary may not transfer pursuant to paragraph (1) any shotgun that is a modular ancillary addition to a service rifle or meets the definition of a “short-barreled shotgun” as that term is defined in , United States Code.section 921(a)(6) of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: The total number of surplus shotguns, including the make and model of each such shotgun, that meet the criteria for transfer to the corporation under such subsection.

children.label: B
children.text: The total number of surplus shotguns, including the make and mode of each such shotgun, that the Secretary concerned intends to transfer to the corporation under such subsection.
In this subsection, the term “Secretary concerned” has the meaning given such term in , United States Code.section 101(a)(9) of title 10

children.heading: .—Limitation on Transfer of Surplus Shotguns
children.label: l .—Limitation on Transfer of Surplus Shotguns
children.text: A Secretary may not transfer surplus shotguns described in subsections (h), (i), or (j), until the date that is 60 days after the date of the submittal of the report required under subsection (k).

children.heading: .—Briefing Required
children.label: m .—Briefing Required
children.text: The Secretary of the Army shall provide to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and the House of Representatives a briefing on the results of the investigation by the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives and the United States Army Criminal Investigation Division regarding unaccounted for pistols at the corporation. The briefing shall be provided after the investigation has concluded.
